questions.question: The functional unit of the kidney is:
Urea is produced in the liver from:
The countercurrent multiplier mechanism operates in:
ADH (vasopressin) acts on which part of the nephron?
Normal glomerular filtration rate (GFR) is approximately:
The juxtaglomerular apparatus secretes:
The diagram shows the structure of a nephron. Which part performs maximum reabsorption of glucose and amino acids?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of nephron showing: Bowman's capsule with glomerulus (filtration), Proximal Convoluted Tubule PCT (major reabsorption), Loop of Henle descending and ascending limbs (concentration), Distal Convoluted Tubule DCT (fine-tuning), Collecting Duct (water reabsorption under ADH). Peritubular capillaries surrounding tubules. Arrows showing reabsorption and secretion

questions.question: Uricotelic organisms excrete:
Aldosterone increases:
Renal calculi (kidney stones) are most commonly composed of:
The diagram shows the process of urine formation. Which process involves movement of substances from peritubular capillaries into the tubular lumen?
questions.diagram_description: Nephron diagram with arrows showing three processes: (1) Filtration – blood pressure forces fluid from glomerular capillaries into Bowman's capsule; (2) Reabsorption – substances move from tubule lumen back into peritubular capillaries; (3) Secretion – substances move from peritubular capillaries INTO tubule lumen (opposite direction to reabsorption). Each process labeled with examples

questions.question: Diabetes insipidus is caused by:
Which substance is freely filtered but NOT reabsorbed, used to measure GFR?
The ascending limb of Loop of Henle is:

options.A: Permeable to water but not solutes
options.B: Impermeable to water but actively transports NaCl
options.C: Permeable to both water and solutes
options.D: Impermeable to both water and solutes

questions.question: Anuria refers to:

options.B: Absence or very little urine production (<100mL/day)

questions.question: The diagram shows the RAAS (Renin-Angiotensin-Aldosterone System). What does Angiotensin II do?
questions.diagram_description: Flow diagram of RAAS: Low blood pressure/volume → JGA releases Renin → Renin converts Angiotensinogen (liver) → Angiotensin I → ACE (lungs) converts → Angiotensin II → multiple effects: vasoconstriction (increases BP), stimulates adrenal cortex to release Aldosterone (Na+ reabsorption → water retention), stimulates ADH release, stimulates thirst center

options.B: Vasoconstriction and stimulation of aldosterone release

questions.question: Creatinine in blood is mainly produced from:
Proteinuria (protein in urine) indicates:
The descending limb of Loop of Henle is:

options.B: Permeable to water but not solutes

questions.question: Dialysis removes waste products by:
The diagram shows a cross-section of the kidney. Which region has the highest osmolarity?
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of kidney showing: cortex (outer region, osmolarity ~300 mOsm), outer medulla (300–600 mOsm gradient), inner medulla (600–1200 mOsm at papilla tip), renal pelvis, ureter. Nephrons shown with glomeruli in cortex, loops of Henle descending into medulla creating gradient, collecting ducts passing through medulla to pelvis

questions.question: Erythropoietin is secreted by the kidney when:
The blood urea nitrogen (BUN) increases in:
Obligatory water reabsorption occurs in:
Which nitrogenous waste is least toxic and requires most water for excretion?
The diagram shows the juxtaglomerular apparatus. Which cells secrete renin?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of JGA showing afferent arteriole approaching glomerulus, juxtaglomerular (granular) cells in the wall of afferent arteriole (contain renin granules), macula densa cells (modified DCT cells sensing NaCl concentration), extraglomerular mesangial cells between the two, efferent arteriole leaving glomerulus. Labels on each component

questions.question: Normal urine pH ranges from:
Haematuria means:
Micturition reflex is initiated by:
The threshold concentration for glucose reabsorption is:
The diagram shows the countercurrent exchange in vasa recta. What is the purpose of this mechanism?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing vasa recta (hairpin-shaped blood vessels) descending alongside descending limb of Loop of Henle. As blood descends: NaCl enters blood, water leaves (osmolarity increases). As blood ascends: NaCl leaves blood, water enters (osmolarity decreases). Net effect: medullary interstitium osmolarity gradient maintained despite blood flow through medulla

options.A: To deliver O2 to tubule cells only
options.B: To maintain medullary osmolarity gradient without washing it away
options.C: To remove waste directly from tubules
options.D: To concentrate urine in collecting duct

questions.question: Renal failure leads to accumulation of:

options.B: Urea, creatinine and other nitrogenous waste in blood

questions.question: Peritubular capillaries are branches of:
Which hormone promotes K+ excretion in urine?
Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by:
The renal corpuscle consists of:
Urea cycle occurs in:
Glomerulonephritis is caused by:
The diagram shows the filtration membrane of glomerulus. Which layer prevents large proteins from entering filtrate?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of glomerular filtration membrane showing three layers: (1) Fenestrated capillary endothelium (large fenestrae, allows most substances through except cells); (2) Basement membrane (fused, negatively charged, size and charge selective barrier blocking albumin); (3) Podocytes with foot processes (pedicels) and filtration slits. Red blood cell shown too large to pass. Albumin shown blocked by basement membrane

questions.question: Urine is transported from kidneys to bladder via:
Parathyroid hormone (PTH) affects kidney by:

options.B: Increasing Ca2+ reabsorption and phosphate excretion

questions.question: Artificial kidney machine (dialysis) principle is:

options.B: Diffusion of solutes across semipermeable dialysis membrane
options.D: Osmosis of water out of blood

questions.question: In the PCT, glucose is reabsorbed by:

options.B: Secondary active transport using Na+ gradient

questions.question: Which of the following appears in urine in uncontrolled diabetes mellitus?
Podocytes are specialized cells of:
The diagram shows changes in osmolarity along the nephron. Where does the filtrate become most hyperosmotic compared to plasma?
questions.diagram_description: Graph of tubular fluid osmolarity vs nephron segments: Bowman's capsule/PCT (300 mOsm, isosmotic), descending Loop of Henle (increases to 1200 mOsm at bottom of loop), ascending Loop of Henle (decreases back to ~100 mOsm – hypoosmotic at macula densa), DCT (variable), collecting duct (increases back to 1200 mOsm in medulla under ADH)

options.B: Top of ascending Loop of Henle
options.C: Bottom of descending Loop of Henle

questions.question: Acute kidney injury most commonly results from:

options.B: Severe dehydration, sepsis, or nephrotoxic drugs

questions.question: The urinary bladder wall is lined by:
Which of the following is NOT filtered at the glomerulus?
SIADH (Syndrome of Inappropriate ADH secretion) causes:

options.B: Hyponatraemia (low blood Na+) due to excess water retention
